# <a id="_AbbreviationsMarker"></a><a id="_Toc1948202623"></a><a id="_Toc200459208"></a>DR and BCP Document Library

[AIS DR Documentation](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/ITCoreDeliveryServices/Shared%20Documents/Infrastructure%20and%20Cloud/Cloud%20Services/Azure%20Integration%20Services/AIS%20DR%20Documentation?csf=1&web=1&e=9XDiQh)

# <a id="_Toc2075022720"></a><a id="_Toc200459209"></a>Related Documentation

Please use this link to access MW established DR and BCP processes such as GEMS & MIM\.

[Melbourne Water \- Emergency Management](https://intranet.melbournewater.com.au/workinghere/Riskresilianceandassurance/emergencymanagement)

<a id="_Toc200459210"></a><a id="_Toc146013523"></a><a id="_Toc145671319"></a><a id="_Toc1152993624"></a># Purpose

A disaster recovery plan \(DRP\) is a structured and documented approach that outlines how an organisation can quickly resume its operations after an unplanned incident\. A DRP is a crucial part of the business continuity plan \(BCP\) for AIS Services\.

 Below are the scenarios for this DRP:

-   Loss of any or all of the Melbourne Water Azure Australia Southeast region services that make up the AIS Services technical architecture\.

<a id="_Toc141105771"></a><a id="_Toc141105734"></a><a id="_Toc200459211"></a><a id="_Toc146013524"></a><a id="_Toc145671320"></a><a id="_Toc511213798"></a><a id="_Toc512348205"></a><a id="_Toc519767701"></a><a id="_Toc245801757"></a># Scope

The scope of this document covers:

- Azure services required to run cross\-region AIS Disaster Recovery
- Data replication processes
- Automatic and manual Failover processes for all AIS services
- Azure resource build processes using Azure DevOps
- Roles and responsibilities during a DR exercise

<a id="_Toc200459212"></a># <a id="_Toc759523661"></a>System Overview

## <a id="_Toc200459213"></a>Overview 

Melbourne Water is transforming its integration landscape by adopting Azure Integration Services \(AIS\) in the production Azure environment\. This cloud\-first approach replaces traditional BizTalk server\-based integrations and establishes a modern, resilient architecture for applications such as TechOne, Pega, and Altus Project Online\.

This implementation leverages a suite of key Azure Integration Services to deliver a robust, scalable, and secure enterprise\-grade solution\. The Azure services used to build this architecture that are required for DR are:

- Logic Apps
- Function Apps
- Service Bus
- Event Hub
- Event Grid
- Storage Accounts
- API Management Services
- Key Vault
- Data Factory

The goal of the Azure Integration Services platform is to allow centralisation of Melbourne Water Azure integrations enable scalability and flexibility of adding more integrations in the future\. The services form a cohesive integration layer that supports both event\-driven and request\-response communication patterns, aligning with modern cloud\-native architecture principles\.

Tier Rating and Recovery Requirements for AIS Services:

RTO

RPO

Tier Rating

24

24

2

# <a id="_Toc11166318"></a><a id="_Toc200459215"></a>Technical Documentation

## <a id="_Toc1462148068"></a><a id="_Toc200459216"></a>System Architecture

The APIM has been newly built as part of the AIS project\. A new Public IP address has been allocated, and appliances such as Web Application Firewall \(WAF\), on\-premises firewall, and Cloud Firewall needs to be updated to allow the new Public IP\. This makes it important to understand the traffic flow and the intervening devices that fall along the way, so that the configuration changes can be documented and executed during migration\.

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)  
The current networking in place will route traffic from Cloudflare \-> on\-premises DMZ \-> ExpressRoute \-> Azure Firewall \-> Azure APIM\.

Web Application Firewall: CloudFlare, Cloud Flare – Ingress, Cisco Umbrella\- MW\-Outbound\.                    

![A diagram of a cloud network

AI-generated content may be incorrect.](data:image/png;base64,)

The diagram illustrates the architecture of the Azure Australia Southeast region, which serves as the primary environment for Azure Integration Services \(AIS\)\. At the core of this setup is the AIS resource group, which hosts shared services such as Data Factory, Logic App and Function App service plans, API Management, Service Bus, and Key Vault\. These components form the backbone of the integration platform, enabling secure and scalable communication between systems\.

Each downstream application, such as TechOne, Pega, or Altus Project Online, resides in its own dedicated resource group\. This design ensures logical isolation and governance while still allowing controlled access to AIS core services\. Applications connect to AIS resources through private endpoints and managed identities, which eliminate the need for credentials and maintain traffic within the Azure Virtual Network for compliance and security\.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## <a id="_Toc508583067"></a><a id="_Toc200459217"></a>Critical Dependencies

Identify all the system specific dependencies for this plan to work\.

This section should outline all the critical information regarding software, server replication, and stand\-up sequence to ensure that the system can be restored within acceptable timeframes\.

Type

Details

__Hardware__

N/A – Entirely Cloud based\.

__Software__

The AIS platform must first be restored or confirmed functional prior to downstream applications \(TechOne, Pega, Altus Project Online\) DR pair activation\. This includes these services:

- Logic Apps
- Function Apps
- Service Bus
- Event Hub
- Event Grid
- Storage Accounts
- API Management Services
- Key Vault
- Data Factory

__Network & Connectivity__

Production region: Azure ExpressRoute

DR region: VPN

Production Firewall: FW\-ase \(Australia Southeast\)

DR Firewall: FW\-ae \(Australia East\)

Azure DNS Private Resolver

Azure Private DNS Zones

Azure Private Endpoints

__Access Management__

Identity management service provided by Microsoft Entra ID\. Users will need to access the following Azure locations:

__Subscriptions: __

- 1\.MelbWater\-PRD
- 0\.MelbWater\-Shared\-Svcs

__Resource Groups: __

- rg\-prd\-ae\-t1
- rg\-prd\-ase\-t1
- rg\-prd\-ae\-pega
- rg\-prd\-ase\-pega
- rg\-prd\-ae\-pol
- rg\-prd\-ase\-pol
- rg\-hub\-ase
- rg\-hub\-ae
- rg\-prd\-ase\-ais
- rg\-prd\-ae\-ais
- rg\-PRD\-network\-ase
- rg\-PRD\-network\-ae

__With the following conditions:__

- Contributor or Global Administrator role
- Key Vault Administrator role
- MFA enabled account

From a Melbourne Water office or through an allowed VPN

__Data__

Azure Storage Accounts in this architecture have all been configured with Geo\-redundant storage \(GRS\) enabled\. This feature ensures data redundancy by replicating it to 3 locations within the same region, and 3 locations in a region outside of the Production region’s data centre\.

__Infrastructure__

Microsoft manage all infrastructure as part of the Azure Cloud services\.

__Upstream__

All services in the Microsoft Azure East region need to be up and accessible to enable DR capability for AIS\. This would include Microsoft Entra ID services to enable user administrative access to the Melbourne Water Azure tenancy\.

__Downstream__

All Azure AIS Services\.

__Integrations__

The following external systems have direct integration dependencies on the AIS platform\. Each must be accessible from the DR region \(Australia East\) for the Altus DR pair to operate end\-to\-end:

| External System | Integration Type | DR Dependency |
|---|---|---|
| **TechOne SFTP** | File\-based \(SFTP — external cloud endpoint\) | External SFTP endpoint `melbwater.sftp.t1cloud.com` must be reachable from the DR region\. TechOne SFTP credentials are stored in Key Vault and must be available in the DR Key Vault instance\. |
| **Inflo** | File\-based \(SMB share via Self\-Hosted IR\) \+ REST API \(Azure Function\) | SMB share at `svftpprod10.mwc.melbournewater.com.au\SFTP_SENSEI_IN_INFLO` must be reachable from DR\. Inflo SMB password must be present in DR Key Vault \(`inflo-smb-password`\)\. InfloDataExport and InfloProjectUpdate Azure Functions must be operational\. |
| **Maximo** | File\-based \(SMB share via Self\-Hosted IR\) \+ Azure Function | SMB share at `svftpprod10.mwc.melbournewater.com.au\SFTP_SENSEI_IN_MAXIMO` must be reachable from DR\. Maximo SMB password must be present in DR Key Vault \(`maximo-smb-password`\)\. MaximoBudgetExport Azure Function must be operational\. |
| **ESRI** | Azure Function \(REST outbound\) | EsriProjectDataExport Azure Function must be operational in the DR Function App\. Outbound connectivity from DR region to ESRI endpoints required\. |
| **BizTalk** | File\-based \(Azure Function polling\) | BiztalkFileTransferProcessor Azure Function must be operational\. BizTalk file drop locations must be accessible from the DR Function App\. |

All five external integrations depend on the DR Self\-Hosted Integration Runtime node being online and the DR Key Vault containing the required credentials\. Failure of any of these systems will result in partial data flow interruption even when the AIS platform itself is healthy\.

## <a id="_Toc200459218"></a><a id="_Toc1139651369"></a>Pre\-requisites for cutover

The Australia East DR region Logic Apps and Function Apps require Azure Storage Accounts that are also based in the Australia East DR region as they cannot connect to the primary Azure Southeast region Storage Accounts\. The pre\-requisites for cutover require resources for Logic Apps, Function Apps, and Storage Accounts to be built in the Australia East DR region with connectivity in place\.

These Australia East DR region\-based resources have already been built and have met the aforementioned pre\-requisites for cutover\.

__7\.4	     Complete Technical Documentation__

__AIS As\-built documentation:__

- __TechOne Handover Documents: __[K10417 TechOne Cloud Transition Project \- Documents \- TechOne Handover Documents \- All Documents](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/sites/K10417TechOneCloudTransitionProject/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FK10417TechOneCloudTransitionProject%2FShared%20Documents%2FGeneral%2FSolution%20Design%2FTechOne%20Handover%20Documents&viewid=427a246a%2D03ce%2D452d%2Daa46%2Da357e3c8277d&csf=1&web=1&e=MjPmBw&CID=acce62f3%2D7699%2D4a7e%2Da1c5%2D6f44bd709954&FolderCTID=0x01200088ED134E0C73BD4B84FA784B819F9CDD)

- [TechOne\_Vago Integration \_AS\_IS\.docx](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/K10417TechOneCloudTransitionProject/Shared%20Documents/General/Solution%20Design/TechOne%20Handover%20Documents/TechOne_Vago%20Integration%20_AS_IS.docx?d=we7da1ae4c8a1409ca408791e30c122f8&csf=1&web=1&e=ZSCfHt)
- [TechOne\_Visa Integration \_AS\_IS\.docx](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/K10417TechOneCloudTransitionProject/Shared%20Documents/General/Solution%20Design/TechOne%20Handover%20Documents/TechOne_Visa%20Integration%20_AS_IS.docx?d=w4210abfdbda14dec83eea9e2547dea04&csf=1&web=1&e=bwjaHX)
- [TechOne\_Westpac Integration \_AS\_IS\.docx](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/K10417TechOneCloudTransitionProject/Shared%20Documents/General/Solution%20Design/TechOne%20Handover%20Documents/TechOne_Westpac%20Integration%20_AS_IS.docx?d=w60502ef0ed334fa9a0af3f1f3a8675bf&csf=1&web=1&e=zRLVDo)
- [TechOne\_ExpenseMe Pro Integration \_AS\_IS\.docx](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/K10417TechOneCloudTransitionProject/Shared%20Documents/General/Solution%20Design/TechOne%20Handover%20Documents/TechOne_ExpenseMe%20Pro%20Integration%20_AS_IS.docx?d=wb628e72f0d4a45dc9a66790c65f61944&csf=1&web=1&e=trQJ3c)
- [TechOne\_eCloud Integration \_SFTP\_AS\_IS\.docx](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/K10417TechOneCloudTransitionProject/Shared%20Documents/General/Solution%20Design/TechOne%20Handover%20Documents/TechOne_eCloud%20Integration%20_SFTP_AS_IS.docx?d=wc724397551e1402da6ab08ea2209f2e2&csf=1&web=1&e=67WDxi)

__Pega Handover Documents:__

- [INT\-328 MW Pega Azure Integration Detailed Design v1\.1\.docx](https://melbwater.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/ITCoreDeliveryServices/Shared%20Documents/Infrastructure%20and%20Cloud/Cloud%20Services/DevOps%20-%20General/Pega/INT-328%20MW%20Pega%20Azure%20Integration%20Detailed%20Design%20v1.1.docx?d=w757ae7d050be4ecc996daa012fbb6bfc&csf=1&web=1&e=vxouXt)

## <a id="_Toc200459219"></a>Installation and set\-up \(Barebones rebuild\)

AIS resources can be deployed through Melbourne Water’s Azure DevOps environment\. The AIS DevOps environment is used for the maintenance of the AIS Azure resources, and core AIS code for Logic Apps and Function Apps\.

The DevOps environment can be located here in the Azure DevOps organisation\.

The core AIS pipelines are stored in a folder called ‘AIS Core’\. These pipelines contain all of the AIS resources that are shared between teams\.

Note: There are some other pipelines that are used by specific teams, but they are not part of the AIS project specifically\.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

The pipeline for each item is kept in the same folder as the item being deployed\. For example, the Key Vault folder is located at: AISServices/main/keyVault, and the Key Vault pipeline is located at: AISServices/main/keyVault/azure\-pipelines\.yml\.

The code is organised into logical folders, each containing a logical part of the TechOne system\.

- __AIS__ – This folder is a git submodule that links back to the AIS repository, containing Bicep templates for various types of Azure resource\.
- __Infrastructure__ – This folder contains the TechOne specific Bicep code\. Each resource that is deployed to the TechOne resource groups has a folder here\.
	- __keyvault__ – TechOne Key Vault for storing TechOne specific secrets\.
	- __logicApp__ – The base Bicep templates for creating integration specific Logic Apps\.
	- __roleAssignments__ – The Bicep code for setting up the role assignments required for the logic apps\.
	- __storageAccount__ – The shared storage account that logic apps will use for their operation\.
	- __storageAccountBusiness__ – The business storage account that stores files that have been transferred by the integration workflows\.
	- __storageAccountSftp__ – A storage account that is configured as an SFTP server to allow some integrations to push files to TechOne remotely\.
- __templates__ – The shared DevOps pipeline code, using DevOps templates\.
- __README\.md__ \- The readme which contains pretty much the same information as this document\.

Similarly for __TechOne__,  
![TechOne Pipelines](data:image/png;base64,)

__Pega__  
![A screenshot of a computer

AI-generated content may be incorrect.](data:image/png;base64,)

__Altus Project Online__

![A screenshot of a computer

AI-generated content may be incorrect.](data:image/png;base64,)

# <a id="_Toc200459220"></a>Recovery Strategy

System owners will work with GEMS process to identify the relevant response strategies, based on the scale of impact or the affected component of the system\.

## <a id="_Toc200459221"></a>Partial DR Strategy

The partial Disaster Recovery Plan will be invoked in the case of an outage of the Azure Australia Southeast region\.

The AIS Service Owner needs to immediately advise IT Support \(e\.g\., Cloud Operations, TCS or IT helpdesk\), who will determine the cause of the failure\.

Document the detailed steps required to restore failed components in the system:

- __Key Vault \(Automatic Failover\)__

Key Vault automatically replicates data from the primary to the secondary region and handles failover automatically during outages\. This process requires no action\. As private endpoints are in use, it may take 20 minutes to re\-establish the connection\.

In an outage, the requests that are made of Azure Key Vault in that region are automatically routed \(failed over\) to the secondary region\. When the primary region is available again, requests are routed back \(failed back\) to the primary region\.

- __API Management Services \(Automatic Failover\)__

Azure API Management has been deployed as a multi\-region deployment, which replicates data and will failover automatically\. The failover process for this service requires no action\.

If the primary region goes offline, the API Management management plane and developer portal become unavailable, but the secondary region continues to serve API requests using the most recent gateway configuration\.

- __Event Grid \(Automatic Failover\)__

Event Grid services are replicated and fail over automatically\. The failover process for this service requires no action\.

Event Grid resource definitions for topics, system topics, domains, and event subscriptions and event data are automatically replicated across three availability zones\. When there's a regional failure in one of the availability zones, Event Grid resources automatically failover to another availability zone without human intervention\. Currently, it is not possible to control \(enable or disable\) this feature\. When an existing region starts supporting availability zones, existing Event Grid resources are automatically failed over to take advantage of this feature\.

- __Service Bus \(Manual Failover\)__

Service Bus failover must be triggered by Melbourne Water and will never be triggered automatically by Azure\.

The Service Bus service has the Geo\-Disaster Recovery feature enabled with a paired region \(Australia East\)\. The feature ensures that the entire configuration of a namespace \(entities, configuration, properties\) is continuously replicated from a primary namespace to a secondary namespace with which it is paired and allows initiation of a once\-only failover move from the primary to the secondary at any time\. The failover move re\-points the chosen alias name for the namespace to the secondary namespace and then breaks the pairing\. The failover is nearly instantaneous once initiated\.

Note: This feature enables instant continuity of operations with the same configuration but does not replicate the messages held in queues or topic subscriptions or dead\-letter queues\. To preserve queue semantics, such a replication requires not only the replication of message data, but of every state change in the broker, which is offered in the Geo\-Replication feature \(Public Preview\)\.

- __Data Factory \(Manual Failover\)__

In the event of a disaster, Azure Data Factory will be deployed to the Australia East region from existing code builds stored in Azure DevOps\. Data Factory data such as blob storage is replicated from the primary region, and the Australia East region deployment will connect to the failed over storage\.

- __Event Hub \(Manual Failover\)__

Event Hub has been deployed with instances in both the primary and secondary regions\. In the event of a region failure, manual intervention is required to connect resources to the Australia East region Event Hub resource \(evhns\-prd\-ae\-ais\)\.

- __Logic Apps and Function Apps \(Manual Failover\)__

These two services have been deployed with instances in both the primary and secondary regions\. They share a dedicated Storage Account in the secondary region and do not require replicated data from the primary region\. In a disaster event, traffic manager will need to be re\-routed to point to the Azure Australia East region resources:

Resource Name

Resource Type

Resource Group

logic\-prd\-ae\-ecloud

eCloud Logic App \(Standard\)

rg\-prd\-ae\-t1

Used for sentinel logs ingestion of TechOne activity/access logs

rg\-prd\-ae\-t1

stprdaeAISlogic

rg\-prd\-ae\-ais

Function App for Altus POL data transformation

rg\-prd\-ae\-pol

- __Storage Accounts \(Automatic Failover\)__

All Production Storage Accounts in this architecture have all been configured with Geo\-redundant storage \(GRS\) enabled\. This feature ensures data redundancy by replicating it to 3 locations within the same region, and 3 locations in a region outside of the Production region’s data centre\. In the case of a region failure, Azure will failover data automatically to the secondary region with no manual intervention required\.

__NOTE__: Use the run sheet to verify successful restoration of the system\. 

## <a id="_Toc200459222"></a>Enterprise/Full DR Strategy

Decision to invoke full\-scale disaster recovery plans will be communicated to system owners through the GEMS process\.

# <a id="_Toc229599193"></a><a id="_Toc200459223"></a>Recovery Runsheet 

System owners or disaster recovery personnel should use the DR Runsheet to validate the successful recovery of the system\. The DR Runsheet is to be used for both planning, testing and actual DR mode\. 

Link to separate Recovery Runsheet:<a id="_Toc141105754"></a><a id="_Toc141105791"></a><a id="_Toc141105765"></a><a id="_Toc141105802"></a><a id="_Toc62196869"></a><a id="_Toc441074694"></a> [IT TEM DR Runsheet \- AIS Services\.xlsx](:%20https:/inflo.mwc.melbournewater.com.au/inflo/cs.exe/properties/70970429)
